expectations.text: The review identifies the test file importing from '../src/internal/helpers' as a test boundary violation
expectations.evidence: Report [Important] tests/user-service.test.ts:2,32-42: 'Tests are coupled to internal implementation details. Evidence: Tests import ../src/internal/helpers directly and validate helper internals instead of public module behavior.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the comment 'Used by the UserRegistration component' as upstream knowledge that will rot
expectations.evidence: Refactoring: 'Removed transient/upstream-coupled comments ("Used by ... pages/register.tsx ...") that encode caller knowledge and will drift.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the 'export * from ./internal/helpers' in index.ts as leaking internal implementation details through the barrel
expectations.evidence: Report [Minor] src/index.ts:3: 'Internal helper module is now exported as public API surface. Evidence: export * from ./internal/helpers exposes validateEmail, hashPassword, generateUserId from an internal path through the package root.'

expectations.text: The review identifies the constructor doing require('pg') and new Client() as a DI anti-pattern
expectations.evidence: Report [Important] src/user-service.ts:29-36: 'Domain service now depends directly on infrastructure (pg) and opens DB connections in constructor... this inverts dependency direction and hard-couples domain logic to Postgres. Recommendation: Inject an AuditLogger interface.'

expectations.text: The report uses the specified severity format
expectations.evidence: Report uses [Important] and [Minor] with file:line, Evidence, Recommendation throughout.
